label: Recursive partitioning & ensembling

node.label: Recursive partitioning & ensembling
node.definition: Decision regions are recursively divided and multiple predictors are combined to reduce variance or bias.
node.why_it_matters: In this atlas graph, Recursive partitioning & ensembling is modeled as depending on Algorithms & data structures, Statistics & experimental design and feeding downstream nodes such as Decision trees, random forests & boosted trees, Self-consistency & best-of-N inference.
examples: atlas downstream link to Decision trees, random forests & boosted trees
atlas downstream link to Self-consistency & best-of-N inference
limitations: Its benefit depends on implementation, scale, data, and hardware; the same mechanism can add complexity without improving the target workload. Its direct dependencies include Algorithms & data structures, Statistics & experimental design.

dependencies.rationale: In this atlas graph, Recursive partitioning & ensembling has a `uses` dependency on Algorithms & data structures because Algorithms & data structures is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.

In this atlas graph, Recursive partitioning & ensembling has a `uses` dependency on Supervised learning because Supervised learning is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.
